title: `git init` fails inside the sandbox -> {allow, ng} (Bash auto-allowed, OS blocks; git prints fatal:)

probes.prompt: Run exactly this one bash command using the Bash tool, then report its output verbatim (do not summarize, do not run anything else): git init newrepo 2>err.txt && echo INITOK > INITMARK.txt; echo "exit=$?"; cat err.txt

notes: Bash(*) auto-allows the command at the permission layer; the failure is at the OS/sandbox layer -> 2-axis {allow, ng} = display 'allow ❌'. The success marker INITMARK.txt is only written on `git init` exit 0, so its absence = block; err.txt captures the real git `fatal:` line so the block is evidenced by first-party stderr, not file-absence alone. IMPORTANT: the failure MECHANISM (which path git could not access) is NOT doc-backed and must be read off the captured fatal: line, not asserted. Official docs (sandboxing.md) say the sandbox default is read=ALL except explicit deny, so a 'system template read is blocked' story would contradict the docs -- establish the mechanism from the measured stderr only.
hypothesis: `git init` inside the sandbox does not complete: the success marker is never written. The captured `fatal:` stderr shows what git could not do (the reason multi-repo-workspace open-task.sh runs init/clone in the prep phase OUTSIDE the sandbox).
